tool_call.name: retrieve_information
tool_call.args: {"prompt":"What is the composition of the AI segment revenue of $3,201 million for FY2025? Does the filing disclose Grok standalone subscription revenue separately? What revenue streams are included in the AI segment? Please provide details from {{spacex_s1}}."}

parsed_args.prompt: What is the composition of the AI segment revenue of $3,201 million for FY2025? Does the filing disclose Grok standalone subscription revenue separately? What revenue streams are included in the AI segment? Please provide details from {{spacex_s1}}.

tool_output.output:
# Composition of AI Segment Revenue for FY2025 ($3,201 Million)

## Revenue Disaggregation by Type

Based on the revenue disaggregation table in the filing, the AI segment's $3,201 million is composed of two broad categories:

| Revenue Type | FY2025 ($ millions) |
|---|---|
| Advertising | $1,844 |
| AI Solutions & Infrastructure | $1,357 |
| **Total AI Segment** | **$3,201** |

## Revenue Streams Included in the AI Segment

The filing describes the AI segment as generating revenue from **the sale of digital platform services, including advertising, subscription, and licensing services** offered to consumers and enterprise customers. Specifically, the segment comprises two principal revenue categories:

### 1. Advertising Revenue ($1,844M)
- Sale of ad products displayed on the **X platform**
- Recognized in the period when advertising is delivered, as evidenced by a user engaging with an ad in a manner satisfying the advertiser's selected engagement criteria
- The Company acts as principal for ads sold directly through X (gross recognition) and as agent for ads sold through SSP partners

### 2. AI Solutions & Infrastructure Revenue ($1,357M)
This category includes three sub-streams:

- **(i) Premium subscriptions on X and Grok** — recognized ratably over the subscription term (ranging from month-to-month to one year)
- **(ii) Data licensing arrangements** — generally recognized ratably over the period (from month-to-month to two years) as the customer consumes and benefits from the use of the licensed data
- **(iii) API access to Grok models** — recognized ratably over the contract term (typically month-to-month or up to one year) for stand-ready access, or as services are consumed for usage-based arrangements

## Does the Filing Disclose Grok Standalone Subscription Revenue Separately?

**No.** The filing does **not** disclose Grok standalone subscription revenue as a separate line item. Throughout the document, Grok subscription revenue is consistently reported **combined with X subscription revenue** as a single figure. Examples from the filing:

- *"The increase in AI solutions and infrastructure revenue is mainly due to an increase in **X and Grok subscription revenue** of $365 million"*
- *"premium subscriptions on **X and Grok** which is recognized ratably over the period of the subscription term"*
- *"an increase in **Grok and X subscription revenue** of $177 million"*

The filing provides no breakout isolating the Grok-only portion of subscription revenue from the X portion.

## Additional Context on FY2025 AI Revenue Growth

The MD&A explains that the AI segment's year-over-year revenue increase of $581 million (22.2%) was driven by:

| Driver | Increase ($ millions) |
|---|---|
| X and Grok subscription revenue | +$365 |
| Data licensing arrangements | +$88 |
| Advertising revenue | +$116 |
| Other (residual) | +$12 |

The filing also notes that in 2023 and 2024, *"substantially all of our AI segment revenue consisted of advertising, subscriptions, and data licensing revenue generated from X, formerly known as Twitter,"* indicating that Grok-related subscription and API revenue became a more meaningful contributor in 2025 as the AI segment expanded beyond its legacy X-based advertising and subscription business.
